name: LTX-2
tagline: Open video that comes with its own soundtrack
description: A DiT-based audio-video model — the first open-weights system generating synchronized sound and picture together, up to 4K at 50fps. The 22B LTX-2.3 (with a fast distilled variant) covers text-to-video, image-to-video, interpolation, camera-control LoRAs, and ships an official LoRA trainer.
why: Speed and audio are the edge: distilled variants run near real time on an H100 and 2-3x faster than rivals on consumer cards, and native synchronized audio puts an open-weights model in territory otherwise exclusive to closed systems.
useCases: Fast stylized video with synchronized audio
audience: Motion designers, app developers, indie filmmakers

setup.requirements: Official Python packages, ComfyUI, or Diffusers; distilled variants from ~8-16GB VRAM, the 22B quality tier 24GB+

license.weights: LTX-2 Community License — freely downloadable, commercial use for smaller entities, company-scale restrictions apply
license.notes: Not OSI-open on the weights side; larger companies need a Lightricks agreement.

limitations: Community-licensed weights with scale thresholds; photoreal humans trail Wan 2.2; frame counts must be a multiple of 8 plus 1.
take: Sound changes everything about generated video — silence was the uncanny part. First open model to figure that out.
